bibliographic_data.title: DiaryPlay: AI-Assisted Authoring of Interactive Vignettes for Everyday Storytelling
bibliographic_data.abstract: An interactive vignette is a visual storytelling medium that lets the audience role-play a character and interact with non-player characters (NPCs) and the digital environment. Yet, the authoring complexity of interactive vignettes has obstructed their adoption in everyday storytelling, which builds on immediacy. We introduce DiaryPlay, an AI-assisted authoring system that generates interactive vignettes from text stories. The Authoring Component visually elicits three core elements (environment, characters, events) through automation and author refinement. The Viewing Component delivers an interactive story to the audience using an LLM-powered Controlled Divergence Module, which allows divergent player and NPC behaviors within the boundaries defined by the author’s intended storyline.

core_content.novelty_claim: DiaryPlay is an AI-assisted authoring system that generates interactive vignettes from text stories, enabling low-effort authoring for everyday storytelling.
core_content.key_methodology: DiaryPlay comprises two main components: the Interactive Vignette Authoring Component and the Interactive Vignette Viewing Component. The Authoring Component visually guides authors in specifying environment, characters, and events through automation and refinement. The Viewing Component uses an LLM-powered Controlled Divergence Module to deliver interactive stories with divergent player and NPC behaviors.
outcomes: The Controlled Divergence module generates believable NPC activities based on both character persona and storyline.
DiaryPlay enables low-effort authoring of interactive vignettes for everyday storytelling while providing engaging viewing experiences.
core_content.full_text_markdown: Everyday storytelling is an intrinsic part of human nature. People share stories digitally, often in a casual manner. Interactive vignettes, which allow audience participation and interaction with characters and objects, have gained popularity but are not yet used for everyday storytelling due to their complexity. DiaryPlay, an AI-assisted authoring system, simplifies this process by generating interactive vignettes from text stories. It includes two main components: the Authoring Component, which visually guides authors in specifying environment, characters, and events through automation and refinement; and the Viewing Component, which uses an LLM-powered Controlled Divergence Module to deliver interactive stories with divergent player and NPC behaviors. A technical evaluation shows that DiaryPlay can generate believable NPC activities based on character persona and storyline, while a user study demonstrates its effectiveness in enabling low-effort authoring for everyday storytelling.
